prose.title: 35n mos
prose.body:
The 35N Military Occupational Specialty (MOS) in the United States Army is known as an Imagery Analyst.

As a 35N, you would be responsible for analyzing images and data collected from various sources such as unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), satellites, and other reconnaissance platforms. Your primary role would be to interpret and assess the content of these images to provide accurate and timely intelligence to commanders and decision-makers.

Specific tasks in this MOS may include analyzing and interpreting imagery, producing intelligence reports and products, conducting target development, and providing battlefield situational awareness. You may also be involved in planning and coordinating reconnaissance missions to support ground operations.

To become a 35N, you would need to complete the necessary training at the United States Army Intelligence Center of Excellence at Fort Huachuca, Arizona. This training includes classroom instruction and hands-on practice in areas such as imagery analysis techniques, geospatial analysis, and utilizing various analysis software and tools.

Overall, the 35N MOS is critical in providing crucial intelligence support to inform military operations and decisions.
